York[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Promoting walking and cycling in England

* [Civil Aviation Authority](/wiki/Civil_Aviation_Authority_(United_Kingdom) "Civil Aviation Authority (United Kingdom)") * [Crossrail International](/wiki/Crossrail "Crossrail") * [DfT Operator](/wiki/DfT_Operator "DfT Operator") * [East West Rail](/wiki/East_West_Rail "East West Rail") * [Great British Railways](/wiki/Great_British_Railways "Great British Railways") * [High Speed 2](/wiki/High_Speed_2 "High Speed 2") * [National Highways](/wiki/National_Highways "National Highways") * [Network Rail](/wiki/Network_Rail "Network Rail") * [Platform4](/w/index.php?title=Platform4&action=edit&redlink=1 "Platform4 (page does not exist)")

* [Air Accidents Investigation Branch](/wiki/Air_Accidents_Investigation_Branch "Air Accidents Investigation Branch") * [Bus Open Data Service](/wiki/Bus_Open_Data_Service "Bus Open Data Service") * [Marine Accident Investigation Branch](/wiki/Marine_Accident_Investigation_Branch "Marine Accident Investigation Branch") * [National Parking Platform](/wiki/National_Parking_Platform "National Parking Platform") * [Rail Accident Investigation Branch](/wiki/Rail_Accident_Investigation_Branch "Rail Accident Investigation Branch")

* [Airports Commission](/wiki/Airports_Commission "Airports Commission") * [Directly Operated Railways](/wiki/Directly_Operated_Railways "Directly Operated Railways") * [Driving Standards Agency](/wiki/Driving_Standards_Agency "Driving Standards Agency") * [Government Car Service](/wiki/Government_Car_Service "Government Car Service") ([Prime Ministerial Car](/wiki/Prime_Ministerial_Car "Prime Ministerial Car")) * [Independent Commission on Civil Aviation Noise](/wiki/Independent_Commission_on_Civil_Aviation_Noise "Independent Commission on Civil Aviation Noise") * [London and Continental Railways](/wiki/London_and_Continental_Railways "London and Continental Railways") * [Vehicle and Operator Services Agency](/wiki/Vehicle_and_Operator_Services_Agency "Vehicle and Operator Services Agency")

(**ATE**) is the [executive agency](/wiki/Executive_agency "Executive agency") responsible for [active travel](/wiki/Active_travel "Active travel") in England and is part of the UK [Department for Transport](/wiki/Department_for_Transport "Department for Transport").[[2]](#cite_note-2) The organisation is an [inspectorate](/wiki/Inspectorate "Inspectorate") and [funding body](/wiki/Funding_body "Funding body") led by [Chris Boardman](/wiki/Chris_Boardman "Chris Boardman"), the first Active Travel Commissioner for England.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
York Press <https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/news/19865997.active-travel-england-will-based-york-bringing-scores-jobs/>

["PM kickstarts £2bn cycling and walking revolution"](https://www.gov.uk/government/news/pm-kickstarts-2bn-cycling-and-walking-revolution). *GOV.UK* (Press release). 28 July 2020. Retrieved 22 January 2022.

["Olympic gold medallist and cyclist Chris Boardman to lead government's new active travel body"](https://www.gov.uk/government/news/olympic-gold-medallist-and-cyclist-chris-boardman-to-lead-governments-new-active-travel-body). *GOV.UK*. Department for Transport. 22 January 2022. Retrieved 22 January 2022.

["£175 million more for cycling and walking as research shows public support"](https://www.gov.uk/government/news/175-million-more-for-cycling-and-walking-as-research-shows-public-support). *GOV.UK*. Department for Transport. 13 November 2020. Retrieved 22 January 2022.
["Active Travel in England - NAO report"](https://www.nao.org.uk/reports/active-travel-in-england/). *National Audit Office (NAO)*. 7 June 2023. Retrieved 3 June 2024.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Active Travel promotion organisation of the UK Government

Active Travel England

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Abbreviation | ATE |
| Formation | Announced 28 July 2020; 5 years ago (2020-07-28) |
| Founded at | York[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Type | [Executive agency](/wiki/Executive_agency "Executive agency") |
| Purpose | Promoting walking and cycling in England |
| Active Travel Commissioner for England | [Chris Boardman](/wiki/Chris_Boardman "Chris Boardman") |
| Chief Executive | Danny Williams |
| Parent organisation | [Department for Transport](/wiki/Department_for_Transport "Department for Transport") |
| Website | <https://www.activetravelengland.gov.uk/> |

**Active Travel England** (**ATE**) is the [executive agency](/wiki/Executive_agency "Executive agency") responsible for [active travel](/wiki/Active_travel "Active travel") in England and is part of the UK [Department for Transport](/wiki/Department_for_Transport "Department for Transport").[[2]](#cite_note-2) The organisation is an [inspectorate](/wiki/Inspectorate "Inspectorate") and [funding body](/wiki/Funding_body "Funding body") led by [Chris Boardman](/wiki/Chris_Boardman "Chris Boardman"), the first Active Travel Commissioner for England.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Active_Travel_England&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

On 28 July 2020, the Government of the United Kingdom announced the establishment of Active Travel England as part of a new cycling and walking plan called *Gear Change: A bold vision for cycling and walking*.[[4]](#cite_note-Cycling_and_walking_plan_for_England-4)

## Functions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Active_Travel_England&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Functions")]

The functions of the organisation are:[[4]](#cite_note-Cycling_and_walking_plan_for_England-4)

* Enforcement of new cycling design guidance by local authorities
* Provide advice to improve scheme design, implementation and stakeholder management
* As of 2023, statutory consultation for [planning applications](/wiki/Planning_application "Planning application") for all developments over 150 dwellings
* Consider applications for funding from the cycling budget (£2bn initially)
* Publish annual reports on highway authorities, grading them on their performance on active travel

## Funding and effectiveness

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Active_Travel_England&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Funding and effectiveness")]

The DfT released £250 million in May 2020 and £175 million in November 2020 to fund active travel infrastructure schemes.[[5]](#cite_note-5) It was reviewed by the [National Audit Office](/wiki/National_Audit_Office_(United_Kingdom) "National Audit Office (United Kingdom)") in 2023.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Active_Travel_England&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Sustrans](/wiki/Sustrans "Sustrans"), UK active travel charity
* [National Cycle Network](/wiki/National_Cycle_Network "National Cycle Network")
* [Cycling England](/wiki/Cycling_England "Cycling England") (2005–2011), former government body

3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** ["Olympic gold medallist and cyclist Chris Boardman to lead government's new active travel body"](https://www.gov.uk/government/news/olympic-gold-medallist-and-cyclist-chris-boardman-to-lead-governments-new-active-travel-body). *GOV.UK*. Department for Transport. 22 January 2022. Retrieved 22 January 2022.
